title: Birria de Chorizo y Carnitas
description: This Mexican dish is a classic fermented, juicy, and indulgent birria recipe featuring chorizo sausage and carnitas served with fresh cilantro, onion, and lime.
instructions: In a blender or food processor, blend 1/4 cup of the lardons with 2 cloves of garlic, 1 tablespoon of cumin, 1 tablespoon of chili powder, and 1 tablespoon of paprika until well combined.
Heat 2 tablespoons of lardons in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chorizo and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the pork shoulder and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the onion, cumin, chili powder, and paprika. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the onion is softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add 1 cup of water to the Dutch oven and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, until the pork is tender and easily shreds with a fork, about 2 hours.
Shred the pork with two forks and return it to the pot.
Stir in the remaining 1/4 cup of lardons, salt, and black pepper.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the birria in bowls, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro, onion, and a squeeze of lime and sour orange juice.
Serve with warm tortillas, such as corn or flour tortillas, for wrapping the birria.
Serve with your choice of toppings, such as diced onion, chopped cilantro, lime wedges, sour orange wedges, and salsa.
